--cd:
Usage: --cd <Directory>
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Set the specified directory as the current working directory
If using ~, it will use the Linux user's home path
If the path starts with a / character, it will be interpreted as an absolute Linux path
Otherwise, the value must be an absolute Windows path
--shell-type: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Execute the specified command using the provided shell type
e.g. --shell-type standard       Execute the specified command using the default Linux shell
--shell-type login          Execute the specified command using the default Linux shell as a login shell
--shell-type none           Execute the specified command without using the default Linux shell
--help: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}View a list of available options and commands in WSL
--debug-shell: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Open a WSL2 debug shell for diagnostics
--event-viewer: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Open the Application view of Windows Event Viewer
--install:
Usage: --install [<distribution>] [Options]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Install Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) distributions for Linux
To view the list of available distributions, use wsl --list --online
--install --no-launch: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Install the distribution without launching it afterward
--install --web-download: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Download the distribution from the Internet instead of the Microsoft Store
--mount: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Attach and mount physical or virtual disks in all WSL 2 distributions
--mount --vhd: Specify that <Disk> represents a virtual hard disk
--mount --bare: Attach the disk to WSL2 without mounting it
--mount --name: Use a custom name for the mount point
--mount --type: File system to use when mounting the disk, defaults to ext4 if not specified
--mount --partition: Partition index to mount, defaults to the entire disk if not specified
--release-notes: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Open a web browser to view the WSL release notes page
--shutdown: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Immediately terminate all running distributions and WSL 2 lightweight virtual machines
--status: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Display the status of Windows Subsystem for Linux
Examples: default distribution type, default distribution, and kernel version, etc.
--unmount:
Usage: --unmount [Disk]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr}}Unmount and detach a disk from all WSL2 distributions
If called without parameters, unmount and detach all disks
--update --web-download: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Download updates from the Internet instead of the Microsoft Store
-v: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Check version information about WSL and its components
--version: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Check version information about WSL and its components
--export:
Usage: --export <distribution> <FileName> [Options]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Export the distribution as a tar file; for standard output, the filename can be "-"
e.g. wsl --export ubuntu "$env:UserProfile\Desktop\export.tar"
--export --vhd: Specify exporting the distribution as a .vhdx file
--import:
Usage: --import <distribution> <InstallLocation> <FileName> [Options]
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym}}Import the specified tar as a new distribution; for standard input, the filename can be "-"
e.g. wsl --import aaa "$env:UserProfile\Desktop" "$env:UserProfile\Desktop\export.tar"
--import --version: Specify the version to use for the new distribution
--import --vhd: Specify the provided file as a .vhdx file instead of a tar file
This operation will create a copy of the .vhdx file at the specified installation location
--import-in-place:
Usage: --import-in-place <Distro> <FileName>
Import the specified .vhdx as a new distribution.
This virtual hard disk must be formatted with the ext4 file system type
-l --all: Including those currently being installed or uninstalled
--list --all: Including those currently being installed or uninstalled
-l -o: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Show a list of available distributions that can be installed
--list -o: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Show a list of available distributions that can be installed
-l --online: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Show a list of available distributions that can be installed
--list --online: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Show a list of available distributions that can be installed
-s:
Alias: --set-default
Usage: -s <Distribution>
{{if($Distro_list){ $PSCompletions.config.sym}else{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr} }}Set a distribution as the default
--set-default:
Alias: -s
Usage: --set-default <Distribution>
{{if($Distro_list){ $PSCompletions.config.sym}else{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr} }}Set a distribution as the default
--set-version: Change the version of the specified distribution
--set-default-version: Change the default installation version for new distributions
--unregister: {{if($Distro_list){ $PSCompletions.config.sym}else{$PSCompletions.config.sym_wr} }}Unregister the distribution and delete the root file system

wsl_core_info.__system: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Start a shell for the system distribution
wsl_core_info._e: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}Execute the specified command without using the default Linux shell
